accuracy.caveat: This is contract agreement on one synthetic event window, not dataset accuracy. Use baseline:plaintext for real event data.

securityParameters.seedPolicy: fixed seed for reproducible artifact generation
securityParameters.securityClaim: No security claim. Educational additive homomorphic arithmetic only; replace with reviewed OpenFHE parameters for real measurements.

cryptoInventory.notes: Inventory names desired roles for the prototype roadmap. The current runnable code uses only educational additive HE.

privacyModes.comparisonBasis: same event window, same public linear score contract

decision.rationale: Use padded sparse batches as the default comparison lane: it reduces exact sparsity disclosure while staying cheaper than dense encrypted windows.

publicActivePositions.label: Public active positions
publicActivePositions.metadataTradeoff: Fastest path, but exact active positions, timing, and event count are visible to compute.

paddedSparseBatches.label: Padded sparse batches
paddedSparseBatches.metadataTradeoff: Hides exact active-event count inside a fixed bucket, while keeping lower cost than dense windows.

denseEncryptedWindows.label: Dense encrypted windows
denseEncryptedWindows.metadataTradeoff: Best of these three at hiding active positions and sparsity, with the highest encrypted workload.

decision.currentOpenFheTarget: The included OpenFHE BFVrns target currently demonstrates the public active-neuron-position sparse path; use this decision object to choose whether future comparison runs should stay public sparse, add padding, or move dense.

modes.label: Public active positions
hides: final class scores until client decrypts
modes.notes: Fastest mode; suitable only when active-position and timing metadata are acceptable to expose.

modes.label: Public active neuron positions + encrypted features
modes.positionPolicy: sorted active neuron positions are public; sorted event feature values are encrypted
metadataLeakage: active neuron identity and time-bin pattern
hides: final class scores until client decrypts
modes.reconstructionResistance: prevents raw sample replay or value inspection by the compute provider, but does not hide public position metadata
modes.notes: OpenFHE BFVrns target mode for sparse sorted-event scoring; use only where public active neuron metadata is acceptable.

modes.label: Padded sparse batches
modes.positionPolicy: active indices are sent inside a fixed-size padded sparse batch
metadataLeakage: upper bound on active event count
hides: exact active event count within the padding bucket
which padded slots are dummy zeros
modes.notes: Middle-ground mode; spends extra encrypted work to avoid revealing exact sparse workload size.

modes.label: Dense encrypted windows
modes.positionPolicy: all feature positions are encrypted every window
modes.notes: Slowest mode; hides sparsity better by encrypting zero and non-zero features alike.

privacyModes.nextMeasurement: Run the same three modes under OpenFHE BFVrns after native OpenFHE is installed.

privacyModeDecision.rationale: Use padded sparse batches as the default comparison lane: it reduces exact sparsity disclosure while staying cheaper than dense encrypted windows.

publicActivePositions.label: Public active positions
publicActivePositions.metadataTradeoff: Fastest path, but exact active positions, timing, and event count are visible to compute.

paddedSparseBatches.label: Padded sparse batches
paddedSparseBatches.metadataTradeoff: Hides exact active-event count inside a fixed bucket, while keeping lower cost than dense windows.

denseEncryptedWindows.label: Dense encrypted windows
denseEncryptedWindows.metadataTradeoff: Best of these three at hiding active positions and sparsity, with the highest encrypted workload.

privacyModeDecision.currentOpenFheTarget: The included OpenFHE BFVrns target currently demonstrates the public active-neuron-position sparse path; use this decision object to choose whether future comparison runs should stay public sparse, add padding, or move dense.

lanes.valueDomain: non-negative integer spike counts and public non-negative weights
packingNotes: keep active indices public or padded according to the privacy-mode decision
pack one class row or one event window per ciphertext before testing rotations
start with ciphertext-plaintext multiplies for public weights and public bias
lanes.firstMeasurement: Compare public active positions, padded sparse batches, and dense encrypted windows under the same BFV/BGV parameter family.
caveats: parameter selection and noise budget must be measured in a reviewed library
public active positions can leak timing and sparsity metadata

lanes.scoreContract: scores = W x + bias after quantization/scale review
packingNotes: pack dense or padded vectors when approximate features are justified
track scale, rescale, and rounding error against plaintext scores
treat CKKS as a comparison lane until the integer contract needs approximate features
lanes.firstMeasurement: Record score drift, classification agreement, latency, and ciphertext bytes against the integer BFV/BGV lane.
caveats: approximate arithmetic changes the integer score contract
classification margins must absorb CKKS rounding error before any stronger claim

nativeComparisonLanes.comparisonBasis: same synthetic 8x8 event window, same public W x + bias contract, native-library measurements when available

lanes.valueDomain: approximate real-valued neural or ML features
preferredWorkloads: shallow arithmetic where small score drift is acceptable
lanes.accuracyMetric: max absolute score error plus classification agreement

lanes.valueDomain: integer scores and encrypted Boolean threshold decisions
lanes.accuracyMetric: exact plaintext score and encrypted decision-bit agreement

caveats: JavaScript toy timings are not native-library performance claims
CKKS is approximate and must report score drift
public active positions leak sparsity and timing metadata unless padded or densified

representationComparison.comparisonBasis: same synthetic raw neural frame, same linear score contract

representations.label: Dense/raw window
representations.caveat: Reference representation for cost comparison; it is not the preferred privacy boundary.

representations.label: Unsorted spikes
representations.caveat: Useful as a pre-sort baseline only; it should not be the canonical model-facing representation.

representations.label: Spatial-sorted events

cryptoInventory.notes: Inventory names desired roles for the prototype roadmap. The current runnable code uses only educational additive HE.

residualRisks: spatial bin activity can leak coarse source geometry
event-count sparsity can leak workload size
time-bin sparsity can leak coarse temporal patterns

representations.caveat: Canonical gateway encoder path in this package; still simulated and not clinical spike sorting.

representationComparison.caveat: All three representations are compared on one synthetic task. This is a representation and operation-count comparison, not dataset accuracy.

spatialClusterReadiness.clusteringBasis: deterministic spatial bins, not learned neural clusters

snnPath.feedContract: discrete event stream keyed by time bin and neuron ID

blockers: need for reviewed parameter sets and implementation benchmarks

caveats: not a trained SNN
not clinical or biological validation
spatial bins are deterministic prototype features
public active neuron positions leak spatial and timing metadata
production cryptography requires real library integration and review

useLanguage: approved event features cross the boundary under privacy and cryptographic controls
encrypted scoring over a bounded event representation
framingGuardrail.note: Bio-digital language is used here for privacy-preserving event intelligence, not medical diagnosis or treatment.
